subject: Public Health
title: SARS: The First 21st-Century Pandemic Scare
description: A short lesson to help you apply SARS: The First 21st-Century Pandemic Scare.

hook.text: In 2003, a mysterious pneumonia spread from a hotel in Hong Kong to five countries in 24 hours.

buildup.text: SARS-CoV-1 was a new coronavirus jumping from civets to humans in Chinese wet markets.

discovery.text: Aggressive quarantine and contact tracing contained SARS to 8,098 cases and 774 deaths worldwide.

twist.text: SARS disappeared—it was contained before it could evolve to spread more efficiently.

climax.text: Scientists warned that a more transmissible coronavirus could cause a global catastrophe. They were right.

punchline.text: SARS was the dress rehearsal. The world didn't prepare.
